mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts signal synchronized real-economy demand expansion, which lifts equity valuations through higher expected cash flows and reduced recession risk over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with an accelerating federal funds rate compress equity multiples by increasing the real discount rate and signaling policy-induced liquidity contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances despite rising CPI and FEDFUNDS, or the combined signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding real estate finance market, measured by rising residential mortgage debt outstanding, directly fuels household wealth effects and consumption spending, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between RSAFS changes and SPX returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 115 on the index scale, supply-side capacity utilization peaks, indicating robust corporate output and margin expansion that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns within a one-month window.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not statistically higher than the unconditional mean when INDPRO crosses above 115, or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer demand and equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to generate a positive information ratio over a 4-year walk-forward period, or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds 250k monthly despite falling UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth signals broad-based economic momentum that lifts corporate profit expectations and equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth accelerates, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all test periods.
